titre: Why NO ONE Plays: Qiyana | League Of Legends
transcription: within League of Legends there's an archetype of Champions that while small has been a consistent threat throughout the game's timeline seemingly regardless of the metag game owing to being capable of potentially the highest amount of abuse in the game ad assassins more specifically midlane ad assassins thankfully they aren't that massive in number but the ones who do exist perennially rank among the most difficult cast members to deal with in the game Talon Zed and Kiana these three Champions have restored history for their extreme carry potential progressively becoming more and more effective the higher up you go despite not being hyper carries many consider them have the ability to do so Talon at one point held the honor for the highest number of First Bloods out of any Champion being responsible for a quarter of all of them at the height of his career right August went on record to admit that BS team intentionally keeps said underpowered for the sole purpose of mitigating how oppressive he can be but what about Kiana what's her stick though she currently registers I think about a 42% pick rate in Masters at the time of writing this video Kiana notoriously struggles to reach anywhere close to that amount below the Apex tiers while she managed an impressive 10% across the board throughout season 11 and 12 since then she's become a pretty rare site which of course caught my attention though she's by no means yor or cogma levels of unpopular for today's wi in place I wanted to analyze Kiana to find out what exactly happened that made her go from a double digit presence to a relatively obscure pick especially considering her class before we get into the video though I want to give a shout out to today's sponsor Marvel Strikeforce they're celebrating their 7th anniversary so if you're looking for something new to pick up for this spring now's a good time to try it out Marvel Strike Force is a mobile Squad RPG filled with every single character from the Marvel Universe heroes and villains you can range your party level them up and gear them out to participate in 5v5 battles and either BBE or PVP like I said they're celebrating their 7th anniversary and to promote that they're doing a massive content launch and special new user campaign to go along with it so this month you can be on the lookout for the new Star chamers team which includes a couple members of the Guardians of the Galaxy like rocket and Gro Marvel Strikeforce also plans to release Nightcrawler and cyclops for free too through their new promo code 7 available until June 30 which also comes with 500 power course and five premium orbs too all of these characters can be thrown into a variety of campaigns like the story mode raids PVP against other players Alliance Wars and more and the game is constantly updated to ensure there's always something you can check out if you're interested in doing so be sure to use my QR code on screen or Link in the description below and remember to use the promo code 7 for a free Nightcrawler and cyclops thanks get to Marvel Strike Force for sponsoring the video but for now back on topic the reason assassins are consistently popular is because they carry the Perfect Storm when it comes to soloq high Mobility high damage low Reliance on teammates and can improve in performance both micro mechanically and macro mechanically the two Central ways to counter assassins are disruption and coordination disruption is primarily fielded by Tank Champions who can make use of their bulk to withstand a front loaded burst of an assassin and then subsequently use their crowd control to disable their pressure for the rest of their team to then capitalize on it coordinations and effort mandated by the entire team rotating and responding as the unit to be mindful of flanks establish fishion control and to fight in such a way so as to protect their carries in every which way possible given the coordination you find in soloq or lack thereof as well as tanks being far less popular in solu due to the Natural Reliance on the team it's the perfect environment for the assassin class to thrive in however it becomes exceedingly difficult for Assassins to get anything going when faced against the team with either a large amount of dueling Champions or the aformentioned disruption and coordination while some may have some more grounds none were as proficient at handling any possible situation that could come their way than Kiana what that apart from her predecessors though the fact that she could blow up five people just as easily as she could blow up one assassins are primarily known for this single Target DPS Kiana on the other hand was able to Nuke the entire enemy team when allowed sufficient preparation thanks to Elemental wrath being an AOE skill shot with a decent amount of coverage Keo was able to throw two highly damaging attacks one after another Elemental wrath ter shaped to reset with the terrain que then throwing it again when Hedon tandem with her ultimate's massive coverage Kiana had enough kill strength to potentially burst all five members of the enemy team in the right scenario making her the first team fight assassin in the game notably team fight ad assassin ad assassins were balanced by the fact that they could only realistically shut down one person kixs Rengar Talon ad Sho Zed even bluing to an extent to enable this they were afforded highly effective situational items that other classes wish they could have like edge of Knight cera's Grudge and Serpent's Fang all of which Kiana made liberal use of moreover they could still purchase regular brucer items if and when appropriate giving them arguably the highest spill flexibility in the game so when you had an ad assassin who could build all these utility items like slowing and Shield breaking and then apply all of them against multiple members of the enemy team Kiana had or should I say still has insane carry potential CC chaining and wasting teams without any assistance if she could neutralize the main vulnerability of Assassins what exactly was she lacking and that made her not as popular as seemingly less capable members like Zed and Talon undoubtedly the biggest 10in facing Kiana is that her incredible 1v9 carry potential is counterbalanced by the equally incredible mechanical execution and presence of Mind required to actually pull it off consistently most of the time the hardest part of playing an assassin is knowing when to go in and then knowing how to get out once you do go in their burst combos are fairly straightforward though for Talon you dive in with Q throw W pop ultimate to get the final attack then Auto to finish them up sets combos tend to be more elaborate but usually you try to slow them down with a shadow e and then line up your shans to hopefully land all of them kixs and rangar jump on their targets and then use their point and click qes to do 90% of the targets Max Health in a Split Second Kiana's rotation on the other hand is exceedingly more challenging not only is it possible for her to miss every single attack in light of her Q's being skill shots but there are so many more layers to her assassination combo that can also radically change depending on where you are for a class meant to be able to One Tap their target regardless of who what where and when Kiana depends on so many different factors her assassination combo can change based on if you're fighting in the river in Lane in Jungle it depends on where she is in relation to her opponent and more importantly where her opponent is in relation to surrounding Terrain and other entities because their Q deals less damage to enemies beyond the first meaning Kiana can lose a large portion of her damage if her opponent just stands behind a minion or another champion assuming every possible factor is accounted for there's still the challenge of Landing the actual combo K's passive and W both have properties that discourage you from carelessly mashing your face on the keyboard much in the same way Riven dust you need to weave Auto ATT attacks in between ability uses automatically presenting our opponent a chance to react and use whatever defensive measures they have at their disposal not only that but you have to pick up different ter shape elements to reset her passive cool down which is a big chunk of her DPS if you pick up two of the same elements back to back you don't reset your passive on your target throughout all of this you have to land two skill shots and also orchestrate the fight so that you can shout the enemy Champion into a wall for the AOE stun on your ultimate K's total assassination combo without the stun takes uncomfortably long to execute for an assassin altogether creating Windows of opportunity for her to fail said combo putting it simply in exchange for having the ability to Nuke the entire enemy team not only is it very difficult to do but it's also difficult to Nuke one person as well making her skill floor prohibitively onerous to meet on paper she's meant to be an assassin but in practice I'd wa she plays more like a skirmisher or a diver in some cases yes you just want to bum rush the opponent and try to H to zero them other cases you want to harass them with a couple green qes ahead of time before all ining them since K's terrain Q deals 60% more damage against enemies below half health so many different factors to consider not only before you do your combo but during your combo as well missing even one component might result in them surviving enough to fight back and unlike other assassins Kiana lacks an easy way out bringing us to the second problem lending further Credence to my theory that Kiana's closer to a diver than assassin is the fact that she's the only one in the game Who lacks the means of Defense though they hope they never have to rely on it every assassin comes with at least one ability that can be used to respond against retaliation in the event their initial combo doesn't finish the job said can blink back to a shadow after casting death Mark Rengar can either rote enemies with an empowered e to run away or use W to heal any damage he takes kixs can go invisible to reposition himself and buy time for his cooldowns to come back up a Col can do the same or temporarily disengage before diving right back in with either e or ultimate Talon can parkour out just like he parkours in even nefi who's notorious for lacking a means of escaping on her own at least comes packed with a heal on her q and a shield on her ultimate to face tank anything she might be struck with Kiana has nothing she has no healing no shielding no one target ability no return Course once you get in there if you fail to kill your target you're easily at risk of exploding yourself unless are good at kiting and remember like I said even if you line up the perfect set of circumstances and execute your combo correctly Kiana has one of the slowest hundred to zero combos out of any assassin giving her Target plenty of time to fight back the two ways she can get herself out to safia are to use a brush or river cu the former giving her temporary stealth to try and Juke her targets and the latter coming with a shortl lasting route to lock down pursuers but taking either of them means you forfeit her to rank you which is essential for her overall damage most other assassins have their defensive ability without paying a cost to to their DPS choosing to Blink back to your death Mark Shadow doesn't make Z's death Mark any less painful Town's wall parkour has no bearing on his DPS hiana the other hand has to choose between killing or surviving if she wants one she forfeits the other the problem is most of the time she can't afford to prioritize survival if she wants to burst her targets piana's Reliance on a Terrain queue is not for Comfort purposes it's imperative that she does without it her F loaded damage profile is actually quite low assassins typically carry enough DPS to burst a squishy Target twice over if they 2,000 health and assassin usually has enough Firepower to do 4,000 giving them a lenium margin for error if in the event they miss an ability or have to burn their defensive option early how many times we see Z Miss around the shans only for him to still kill you anyway or Talon missing the return trip of rake and still nuking you Rengar using verocity on and power W to cleanse but he still one Taps you that's because they usually have a lot more raw power facted into their kit guess who doesn't Kiana Kiana's base damage and AD scaling per ability are lower to counterbalance the number of different sources of damage in her kit she can use her passive twice Q twice we and ultimate seven sources but the more DPS sources you have the more it becomes necessary to land all of them for the sake of argument let's compare Talon with Kiana as of version 15.4 combining all of Talon's attacks together the maximum damage he can do at level 18 is 11 27.5 base plus 6 90% bonus ad Kiana on the other hand when combining everything does 1264 base plus 5 29% bonus ad and also 10% Max Health damage this assumes you do the full combo perfectly and po the extra damage on a w three times roughly run the same damage as Talon if not slightly less except Talon's combo is close to unavoidable and can happen anywhere instantaneously Kiana needs to factor her position her opponent's position and any potential chance the opponent can defend themselves during the far longer ability rotation time once more the trade-off is that she can potentially apply most of her kit to multiple members of the enemy team but if your goal was to play for a team fight I don't think Kiana would have been your first choice you might as well play Someone Like y aana yaso or Riven who is more consistent team fight pressure Kiana's main problem in relation to her peers is that her pressure range is far too wide with not a high enough ceiling for it to justify the time and effort required to player for most people especially when you can Master someone else in a fraction of the time with only marginally less output in her defense she wasn't always like this Kiana used to be like her other assassin friends who could blow you up and then blow you up again if by some miracle Stroke of Luck You healed back to full due to her extreme dominance during the Mythic item era Kiana received nurse targeted specifically had the need to land everything a few years back she was able to one-hot her combo just from her ability you sigil look no need to even multiple Auto attacks but then there were three key patches that tightened her margin for error first being 11.22 lowering the bonus ad scaling of her audacity by 20% edge of exall would get the 15% lost ad back in the future patch then 12.15 Royal privilege lost 15% bonus ad scaling which when accounting for the 12.4% earlier that year taking 10% already meant that her passive lost almost half its ad scaling forcing into to attack twice with it to make up the difference finally 14.16 even though Kiana got her Q scaling back her ultimate got slammed with a massive Nerf losing 45% bonus CD scaling to it altogether these neres greatly diminished Kiana's frontload of damage you know the thing assassins are known for extending an Assassin's attack Rotation by even half a second is a big deal half a second longer gives you time to bury her your tank support time to peel her off you it gives you time to flash an important attack or it even gives you time to just use any defensive abilities you might have if at any point Kiana were to miss an ability she would basically be at the mercy of her team bailing her up that's assuming she has a chance to reach a point of assassination to begin with assassins are not known for the strong laning face the last thing they want to do is take on someone without the element of chaos or surprise but even then they usually have a way to handle laning face in some capacity now Fury can poke with dark and dagers tal and Z can weave clear with their respective abilities others might have a shield or some way to mitigate damage Pana on the other hand does not first off her level one is arguably the weakest in the game for a carry champion her W can function optimally with that AQ and vice versa then she also has a poor neutral game grass QQ can be helpful against any auto attackers but the base damage on it is so low she has to throw like 10 of them before it poses a threat early game one more issue is that Kiana's damage doesn't really ramp up until you get a few more points in her kit assassins can typically start looking for trouble as soon as they acquire their ultimate but the exal early game is so weak she gets bullied by anyone with the neutral game or Allin game jungle Kiana is not much better either Predator junglers can hunt her down and waste her before she gets a chance to scale and her clear speed is suboptimal until tiam she just doesn't have enough tools to survive the early game comfortably and with how snowballing the game has become players don't want to have to sit through 15 minutes of punishment for only 5 to 10% better performance than someone else in the same class so after taking all this into consideration why does Kiana average the highest win rate of all three assassins at High ELO simple payoff is payoff even if arbitrarily it's only 10% more than what Talon rangar nairi Kix said and someone can do it doesn't change the fact that 10% s 10% Kiana is one of the only two assassins in the game who can wble combo the entire enemy team by herself Mastery over her different Elemental cues can also make her much more flexible than her Pierce even if it's at the expense of damage she has a lot of utility in her K overall from the AOE crowd control on her River CU to the full knockback and stun on her ultimate so unlike other assassins who usually just have to play in One Singular way Kiana is a lot more Dynamic obviously that means it takes a trained eye to know what part of her is the best course of action at what time but the payoff is definitely there it's kind of like a Zer where if you do get over that skill floor there is a payoff it's just most players would much rather pick someone more accessible and consistent in essence those who do play Kiana are those who are really good at her which is why she has a measurably higher win rate you don't have noobs tanking her win rate the same way they do for town or set personally I'm glad she's not popular I was there when Kiana had a 15% pick rate and I never want to go back watch any bayong montage and you'll see what Peak Performance Kiana looks like she better be hard to pick up if that's what you can do on her anyway what do you guys think of Kiana do you agree or disagree with my points feel free to share in the comments that's it for today so if you enjoy the video a like And subscribe would be much appreciated don't forget to follow me on Twitter at fars join my Discord server and check out my other why no and Place episodes if you haven't yet but till next time thanks so much for watching and I look forward to seeing you again soon take care [Applause] [Music] [Music]

titre: How to Play like a ZED MAIN! - ULTIMATE ZED GUIDE for Season 12
transcription:
Hey everyone. Zed is one of the most popular 
champions in the game, and for good reason. Hes   extremely fun, and has a super high skill ceiling. 
This just means he has a ton of potential and   almost every fight you could have done something 
better. He is very difficult though, especially   if you dont have a lot of zed gameknowledge. But 
fear not, in this video were going to teach you   what you need to know to play zed like a Zed main.
Were going to start by covering his playstyle,   then powerspikes, Combos, tips and tricks, 
then finally, runes,build and skill order.  So, lets get started.
Zed is of course, an assassin,   but he is different than a lot of the other 
assassins since he actually plays more like   an AD - Mage, or an AD - Caster as some would 
call him. This is because you normally want   to be using your abilities from range to poke 
and damage the enemy before actually going in.  But zed still does what other assassins 
do, mainly looking to burst an important   target by flanking and catching them off guard.
So lets quickly talk about Zeds power spikes. His   levels 1 and 2 are pretty bad, and he can actually 
start lpaying the game around level 3. But hes a   scaling assassin, meaning the longer the game 
goes, the better it gets for him. Hes actually   the best scaling assassin in the game, excluding 
kassadin, but hes almost as good as him too.  So his first big spike is level 13, where 
he has all of his points in Q and E,   and his level 2 ult. THis makes his E cooldown 
very short and his Q doesnt cost as much energy.  The next big spike is level 16, because with 
ultimate hunter, which you should be brining   every game, since his ult gets down to around 
a 20 second cooldown. Thats obviously really   crazy considering how strong zeds ult can be.
But now, lets get into the fun stuff, combos.  Zeds most important combo by far, is his W E Q 
combo. There are multiple important parts to it.  First, you want to be using W first, and E 
immediately after as the W is travelling, this   makes it so as soon as the W lands, it uses E.
Second, you want to be throwing your W to one   side of them, not directly on top. By doing this, 
the Q coming from your shadow will always hit   because its right next to them. It also makes 
them start running away from the shadow,   so it makes the second Q easy to land as 
well. It looks like this when done right.  I throw the shadow on the bottom side 
of the vlad, then put my mouse on him   and throw my Qs, and they both land.
Now, landing this combo consistently   can easily be the difference between winning 
and losing a game, so you really have to focus   on it. This is the #1 priority combo to 
start learning if you want to play zed.  Something a lot of players dont know about zed 
is, if you land both Qs, you get energy refunded.   This is very important when going for all-ins 
so you have the energy to finish them.  Now that you know the main combo, 
lets expand a bit and look at the   first kill combo once you have your 
ult. This combo is used mainly in lane,   if the enemy mid ever lets you walk into ult 
range without having to use your W first.  You want to walk up and ult, then W E 
Q like this, looking to land triple Qs.  Now, a very very common mistake for low elo 
zeds, is that as soon as they come out of ult,   they use E. You dont want to do that 
because if you hit somone with a shadow E,   it slows them. And if you hit them with a shadow 
E plus your own E, it slows them even more. So,   by ulting and then using W E Q on top of them, 
they will be slowed making your Qs easy to land.  Another variation of this, is super 
super important for teamfights.  A lot of the time it can be hard to get to 
backline safely. So, you will want to ult somone   in the front, then W E Q a carry in the back. 
By ulting somone in the front, you get multiple   benefits. First, it gives you a really nice shadow 
position to escape to, second, it makes the carry   that you are actually targetting, feel very safe.
In this example, Im chasing the enemy Ori,   but my actual target is Cait, so I run at the 
Ori untill cait is in range, Ult the Ori, use the   bread and butter W E Q combo on cait and an auto 
to finish her off, then clean everyone else up.  Theres another benfit of comboing this way as 
well, and its that you get a ton of AOE damage   because you have 3 shadows basically lined up. 
So if you watch how I opened up on this fight   again, I basically had 2 or 3 Qs hitting 
everyone here, which is a ton of AOE dmg.  Alright now were going to get a bit more advanced.
This next combo is for more dangerous situations,   especially ones where you have to be able to 
immmediately swap after ulting. Zed cant swap   to his ult shadow for .5 seconds after using 
it, and you can easily get CCed in those,   so this combo helps a lot for that. Also, its 
nice if you need to kite them out and not get hit.  So I have a few clips here to show you how this 
one goes. the important part is dropping W first   in a safe spot, then R them, immediately 
swap, press E for slow and then use Q.  This one I do the same thing, baiting 
this very fed tristana to jump on me.   I W behind me, then do something a bit advanced 
that will take you some time to get down.   I use my Q and swap to W at the same time, since 
your Q makes you stand still. This makes it so   you are eseentially moving while standing 
still if that makes sense. Its very smooth.  Trist tries to push me away, but because I 
only swapped to my W, I still have my R shadow.   So I then swap back to my R and hit her with my 
passive auto to finish her for the shutdown kill.  Alright so those were 2 combos 
against ranged champions.   Now were going to mix up that same combo 
slightly for the melee ones, especially bruisers.  Bruisers will kill you extremely fast if you are 
in melee range of them. You basically cant let   them touch you. So, the combo, is using W first 
when they are a bit further away like this...  Now if I just swap to that W, I 
lose all of my options to kite.   So, Im going to wait for him to walk away from the 
shadow, then ult him and immediately swap to my W.  So now I have already created distance between 
us, and if he gets on me with flash or Q,   I can still swap back to my ult shadow.
So I hit him with Qs and kite away from his E,   then W in quickly for one more auto and 
swap to my R, giving him no chance of   hitting me because this is an extremely 
fed jax and if I die he can end the game.  He ends up living, just barely getitng 
away from my Qs, but the concept here   is kiting by using W first, then ulting and 
having an immediate swap to continue kiting.  Alright so those are the main zed combos, 
and learning how and when to use which   one is important and will take some time, 
but lets move on to some tips and tricks.  The biggest tip I can give when it comes to zed 
is, you have to understand how important his W   is. Most of zeds skill expression is in his 
W. It creates so many different options and   if its down, you have to respect that.
It also means you really have to pay   attention to how much time you have on it after 
throwing it out. This clip is a perfect example.  Im farming bot lane, and I throw my W E 
Q out to kill the wave. But as I do that,   I see Im getting flanked by the enemy team.
I have to think quickly here because if I   wait untill my W is gone, I wont 
have any way to outplay this.   So, while my W is out, I ult nidalee and 
immediately swap to tht W all the way down here.  Now Ive created a ton of distance between 
us, and my W cooldown is ticking again.  Right before my ult shadow dissapears, I swap back 
and my W is back up so I can W away to safety.  So if I didnt ult nidalee while my W was up, 
I would definetly die as I wouldnt have many   options. You need to know when you use W you 
are on a timer and have to get the most of it.  In laning phase, if W is down, you have to be 
extremely careful of jungle ganks or trades   in general as you cant win many without it. 
Your W is your most important ability on Zed.  Alright another tip we have for zed which 
is super important, is understanding how   strong his passive auto is. Your passive auto 
does a % of their max health as magic damage,   and adds so much damage to your ult. A lot of 
the time, if you can ult them and get a passive   auto off, they are easily in kill range. 
It catches players off guard all the time.  In this clip, Galio jumps in to trade with 
me, and Im trying to fight him but he starts   running away... But I know because of my passive 
auto, I have enough damage to kill him here.   So I Q flash auto E and ignite, and I 
only needed 1 tick of ignite to kill,   thats how much my passive auto did.
Alright, now lets move to Runes   builds and skill order.
For skill order, you want to grab Q then E   then W. And max them in that order as well.
For rune pages, I like to run this one   agaisnt mainly squishy comps. But it 
can be ran every single game just fine.  Against beefy comps with a lot of 
melees like sylas mid aatrox top   Sejuani jungle etc. I like to run this page...
And for builds, zed has a few options this season.  A build you can pretty much go every game is 
eclipse into black cleaver, then more lethality   items. But there are other viable builds as well.
Prowlers Claw and Duskblade are both viable   mythics but you have to learn how to use both 
of them effectively. Prowlers is nice for that   one extra gap closer, its not really used 
for damage on zed. It lets you save W for   more important scenarios or if your W is down 
you can still chase somone that gets away.  Duskblade is amazing for teamfighting if you 
can get the resets, but if you cant kill anyone,   its useless. If you rush duskblade or prowlers, 
I recommend going more lethality second   or a last whisper if snowballing. You are going 
to want last whisper third or fourth item at the   latest most of the time. If the enemy stacks 
armor you just wont deal damage to them. But   mainly on zed you just need to build lethality 
and decide which utlity is best for that scenario.   Edge of night is good if they have immediate CC 
so you can jump in and survive long enough to R   away. Serpents fang is great vs sheilds. 
Youmuus is good vs high movespeed comps, etc.   Its mainly just making sure you know when to build 
last whisper then choose your lethality items.  Alright guys so now you have the tools 
you need to play like a zed main.   Dont be discouraged if you cant do well right 
away as zed is one of the hardest champions in   the game. But if you play him enough and make some 
sick plays, it will all be worth it in the end!   But thats going to bring us to the end of this 
one, we hope you enjoyed and thanks for watching!

titre: The ONLY VEX Guide You'll EVER NEED - League of Legends Season 11
transcription: [Music] what's going on summoners welcome back to another pro guides video i'm kangas and today's video will be our guide to vex the gloomist vex has been out on pbe for quite a bit now and we've been grinding away testing things out so that you don't have to in this video we're going to cover her abilities how she works and of course all the build paths and i do want to note that some people have been trying to make vex work as the support but in all of our testing unfortunately she's more suitable to being a mid laner you can always make vect support work but if you have to make something work there's usually other options that either do it better or more consistently so from here on out this info is aimed specifically at vex mid and remember that since this is all based on info from the pbe it's still tentative but she should be in a similar state on release once she comes out now in case you aren't familiar with vex's kit we'll start out by talking about what she does her passive is doom and gloom the first part about this passive causes vex to periodically gain doom a buff that causes her next basic ability to knock down and fear all enemies hit the duration of the fear scales with levels and while feared the enemies are slowed based on their initial distance from vex the second part of the passive causes nearby enemies the dash blink or get hit by her e to be marked with gloom for six seconds max's next basic attack q or w against a mark's target will consume the mark to deal bonus damage and then refund 25 of doom's cooldown if vex uses a basic attack to trigger this mark it becomes a non-projectile meaning that he cannot be blocked by wind wall or other similar abilities also when attacking non-champions gloom does less damage and refunds 10 percent of doom's cool down her q is mistral bolt vex launches a wave of mist in the target direction dealing magic damage to enemies that it passes through this skill shot starts out slow and wide but after traveling 500 units it gets much faster and narrows in width kind of cool and unique w is personal space where vex emits a shock wave around herself gaining a shield and dealing damage to all nearby enemies her e is looming darkness where she commands a shadow to move to the target location increasing the radius of the area as it travels upon arrival shadow deals magic damage to all nearby enemies and slows them for two seconds and her r is shadow surge where vex launches shadows in the target direction granting sight in the area along the path and dealing magic damage to all enemies hit shatter stops upon colliding with an enemy champion marking them for 4 seconds and revealing them for the duration shadow surge can be recast as long as the target is marked upon recasting shadow pulls vex towards the marked target with displacement immunity dealing damage and consuming the mark once she arrives if the marked target dies within 6 seconds of being hit by shadow surge vex can cast it again with no mana cost for the next 12 seconds that's right we got another resetting ultimate on a death it's kind of been a trend lately but with that out of the way let's do a quick kit summary for vex vex is technically classified as a burst mage but with her kit giving her the ability to dash into opponents and blow them up from close range she's definitely what we would call a battle mage but that isn't to say that she's a bruiser yeah she can get up close and personal but she's not meant to face tank a ton of damage you want to get in use your w shield to mitigate some damage and blow up your enemies before they can kill you she does have a bit of poke damage with her cube but most of the time you're looking to burst out bad guys vex is probably going to start out with a negative win right when she's finally out on the life servers but don't let that discourage you pretty much every champ goes through that when they first come out and once people get past that learning curve they end up being somewhere between pretty strong and really op personally i think vex is a champ that pushes to the op side of things and will be a really dominant pick once people catch up on what she's really capable of but if you want to be one of the first people that gets good at vex this guide is a great starter to put you ahead of the curve but of course if you want to be a true master i highly recommend that you head over to proguides.com our challenger level coaches can teach you all you need to know in every detail and a lot of them have been grinding her on the pbe as well so they're gonna be ahead of the curve too they're available 24 7 so it's never bad time to come and try one out but for now let's talk about how to actually play her starting with the early game many mages have struggled for pretty much this entire season due to them struggling against the mobile bruisers and assassins that dominate mid lane but vex is pretty much perfectly built to deal with those threats the key to this is the doom portion of her passive the ability to fear an aggressive opponent not only allows you to get some distance between the two of you but also sets you up to land her hard-hitting combos any other basic spells can proc this but when you're dealing with a dangerous opponent especially a mobile one that can dash or blink to dodge a skill shot the best thing to do is wait for them to make the first move when they go in you simply use your w this is the most foolproof way to fear an opponent since it's a near instant cast in a decent big circle around vex then you just combo enq and back off until you have your w again since you rely heavily on your fear to disengage scary opponents you want to be mindful of how you're farming the minion wave if you're just spamming q to shove you'll then end up wasting your fear on a minion wave and that is not ideal against less dangerous opponents like other mages you'll rarely be in range to use your w unless you're ulting in so when you have your fear up against them you'll want to lead with your e since q is a pretty telegraphed ability after landing e cue and make sure to move up and use your gloom and powered auto against them to proc electrocute but regardless of what you're laning against once you reach level six you have a lot of kill potential start by trading with them a couple of times to soften them up and then you'll look for an all in after next as well can be pretty hard to hit on its own so try and use it after hitting your fear you could try to fear by landing your e but if you're confident that you have the damage to kill your opponent a guaranteed way to land everything is to lead with a flash w and then quickly ult and e at the same time while they're feared you don't want to use your q right away when you do this combo that's because in the early game the fears a pretty short duration and your opponent may be able to flash out of it instead use the second part of your ult and then use your q using it at melee range causes it to instantly do damage so this guarantees your hardest hitting spells will land on your opponent but once you get to the mid to late game stages of the game vex is a surprisingly well-rounded champ that brings a lot to a team comp most bursty battle mages don't really have safe wave clear and even if they do have aoe damage they have to get pretty close to use it but vex is able to nuke waves safely from a pretty good distance with her q and e when it comes to sieging she's kind of similar to lux because she's got good poke via q and if you land cc on a squishy opponent you can probably burst them down in a single combo but sieging is not your main goal as vex you want to be doing one of two things either going for pigs on squishy targets or blowing up the entire enemy team in team fights for assassinating lone squishies most burst mages rely on some sort of a mistake on the enemy's part either they over extend while farming the side lane or you sit in a brush and they face check you those things certainly work for vex but what she has over other champions in her class is her potential kill range vex assault has between two and three thousand range depending on the rank so if you can snipe an opponent with it you can fly in and land your combo to instantly kill them when somebody knows vex is there dodge and deal is pretty easy but when it comes to flying in from off screen out of the fog of war that can be pretty hard to react to this makes it fairly easy to get consistent picks with this pick and when it's time to team fight you want to be pretty conscious of your cooldowns of course there will be exceptions but generally speaking you'll always want to have your w and your fear available before flying into the enemy team and for that same reason you don't always have to chain your olds right away remember that you have a full 12 seconds window to use your next ult if you get the reset so sometimes it's worth waiting a few extra seconds for your cooldowns to come back up and one last tip is a vex's old is considered a really fast dash kinda like akali z it makes her immune to displacement so she can't be knocked out of it so it's really useful as a means to buffer incoming cc that said she's not untargetable so you can still take damage and be killed while using it now to finish things off let's take a look at the runes that you should be running and the items that you'll want to build for runes you'll run electrocute cheap shot eyeball collection ravenous hunter conditioning and overgrowth with the stat runes mean attack speed adaptive force and either armor or magic resist for secondary runes you could optionally go for transcendence and gathering storm for huge skill and power but in general i recommend the resolve tree because vex is all about getting into the fray and those runes just help out a lot with a team fight survivability for your items you'll start with a doran's ring and then build into luden's tempest and sword shoes after that go horizon focus and either cosmic drive or zoni's hourglass after that you'll want void staff and rabbit on seth cap vex's item build is pretty strict so we recommend that you pretty much always stick to this exact build the reason is that a lot of ex's power and fights comes from getting old resets if you don't have the damage to burst somebody down odds are you're just gonna ult in and get popped the one part that is optional though is whether to go cosmic drive or zonia's hourglass since you don't get ability haste anywhere else in the build aside from your mythic cosmic drive giving you 40 is huge also aside from her old vex has no mobility so the extra movement speed it gives is pretty nice for that reason it's definitely my preferred default item but hey you can go zones when it's absolutely necessary dealing with cops where ulting in would be really risky and there is one alternative build that you can go for but you'll only do it when you're against a very tanky enemy team comp one that has at least three tanks juggernauts or hp heavy bruisers in that case your rune page is arcane comet manifold band transcendence gathering storm eyeball collection and ravenous hunter with the stat runes being attack speed adaptive force and armor or mr for items here you'll go leandres sword shoes cosmic drive demonic embrace void staff and rabbitons since you won't be one-shotting people with your combo this build emphasizes damage over time with the lower cooldowns and burn damage that you can utilize and that wraps things up for our guide to vex the gloomist she's a nice addition to the mage lineups and she works more like a bursty battle mage rather than a control mage that tend to be a bit stale when they're in the meta she will have a bit of a learning curve so don't expect her to have crazy stats when she comes out but i promise she is worth putting some effort to learn and one last thing before you go i want to remind everybody to go check out our community discord if you haven't already we'd love to have you come join us in our conversations that's it for today so best of luck on the rift everybody stay hydrated i'll see you in the next one [Music] you

titre: Laning Phase Just Changed Forever (and nobody even realises)
transcription: Riot just changed laning phase forever because they implemented this change in the new patch notes. And the truth is this actually affects all the lanes. So in this video, I'm going to be showcasing you guys what the changes are and how you can actually abuse it to get an edge over your lane opponent and start winning lanes. So first to understand how these changes directly affect the laning phase, we need to understand how minions work. So minions have a specific targeting system depending on a priority list. The highest priority is champions attacking a different champion. When you out a different champion, you can see all these minions running straight at you. Now, the interesting one in this one is number five because enemy champions attacking an allied minion used to be able to generate minion aggro, but with the changes that Riot made, this number five now completely gets killed. So in some situations, what would happen in the past is that even though that enemy minions attacked an ally minion, which is a higher priority priority than number five, you could cheat this aggro system by sometimes attacking a minion before and therefore drawing aggro and completely changing the way that the lane actually plays out. So there's two key situations where these changes actually matter for your laning phase. The first situation is in the level one because level one minions actually used to behave very differently as to all of the other minions in the game. And the other situation is actually uh whenever you have a minion wave under the enemy turret. titre: 3 Minute Maokai Guide - A Guide for League of Legends
transcription: why play Malai support well he brings tons of CC he is very tanky for a support he's amazing at engaging and he's also brilliant at keeping his carrier Al however he is weaker in the early game and he is very dependent on getting into team fights and his team following up his engages Malai passive is periodically his next basic attack heals him in addition the cooldown of the ability is reduced each time he casts an ability each time he hits an enemy Champion or epic monster with his e or when he struck by an enemy's ability malos Q has two hit boxes firstly a short-range WID skill shot and secondly in a ccle around him enemies hit by this ability are dealt damage and slowed however if enemies are hit by the circle around him they're also stunned and knocked back maai W is a point and click ability which causes him to dash to the enemy becoming untargetable before arriving dealing damage and rooting them Malai e causes him to throw a sapling on the floor which grun sight of the area upon Landing it will last for 30 seconds and if it sees a nearby enemy it will chase them for 2 and 1 half seconds upon hitting an enemy or expiring it explodes dealing damage and slowing enemy hit if the sapling is placed in a brush instead it will last longer and it deals double the damage over three bursts and even slows them for longer as well as revealing them maai ultimate is a massive wide-range skill shot it creates a wall of five Thorns which slowly advance in a direction accelerating over time when they hit an enemy Champion it deals damage and Roots them with the route being longer the further the ability has traveled in addition the first time it hits an enemy Champion maai gains a massive burst of movement speed in addition where this ability is slow moving enemies can move out of it however if you want to use this in a more guaranteed way using it at a right angle to the champion you want to hit is a great way to get it off immediately for combos a great go-to is to use your e into your W followed up by an auto attack into your q and your engage combo is to use ultimate into your W to follow up the CC after this use your auto attack into your q and d for matchups Maly does great into Champions who he can easily engage onto and easily peel off his carry and he can struggle against Champions who can poke him out of Lane and stay you don't then listen up in a lot of fights akali is all about spamming q and passive Auto procs over and over again the thing is to do this you need to constantly weave in and out of range of your opponent that's where buffering comes in whenever you press Q on a Target during your animation you want to have already clicked away this way akali doesn't waste a single moment and immediately begins moving back then when you Auto attack with your passive during that animation you want to be spamming Q so that the ability comes out as soon as your auto is over so it should look smooth like this Q buffer back passive Auto buffer Q buffer backwards and repeat it sounds complicated but in reality it's super easy to do and most players instinctively do this anyway do it properly though and you will immediately see how much smoother your gameplay becomes and your DPS will Skyrocket because of it now here comes the biggest tip of them all you will realize that akali is one of the easiest champions in the game to get kills on if you just learn her re combo so as many of you know akali's e is a literal nuke the second part for some reason is where a huge chunk of her damage is problem is landing it can be a bit inconsistent especially when things get chaotic here's where the re combo comes in it makes it nearly impossible to miss your e best part it's really easy to do you just are keep your cursor on your Target and spam your e while you're dashing to your team then you absolutely murder them the reason this is super important too is because new players take forever to deal damage when killing targets the beauty of the re combo is that it sets up a ton of bursts instantly with zero skill required not only do you guarantee that your e-lands but it immediately sets all your burst up your passive is procked so now you can E2 passive Auto Q into a massive R2 burst it's why this was one of our two most important tips it's so easy and consistent to pull off that you'll be using this combo for nearly every single one of your solo kills and your kill count will rise drastically the final thing we should cover is that the combo won't really work if you try to use R when you're already too close to your targets that's because you won't throw your e until you've flown way past your target this combo works best when you are from further away that way akali shoots her E when she's on top of them making it nearly impossible to miss great with those two critical tips covered let's break down some more tips to make your akali much more consistent most of akali's skill expression can often come from her e alone so let's focus on optimizing it first the immediate thing that any akali player will notice is how frustrating it is that your e forces you to travel on a straight line towards your opponent this can often make it really easy to interrupt and outplay getting you killed so what do you do the first option you've got is the simplest outplay which is to run in a straight line towards an opponent who has a skill shot like that when running directly at someone they'll usually think that you're an easy target to hit this can potentially bait their critical spell then you e Dodge their ability and while they're stuck in their spells animation it also guarantees that your E1 lands of course it won't always be that simple to bait you have a second option with your flash when you use E2 and you're flying towards someone you can actually Dodge their spell by flashing on top of them for the damage this is really useful versus a ton of Champions like Lux Ari Morgana Etc the final option is knowing that activating zanyas won't stop your e2's travel or damage there's literally nothing your opponent can do to stop you from e-tooing them if you've got a stasis cooldown available not only is it useful for avoiding CC but remember your E2 does high burst so being able to go in with it even when you're low is a really great tip to keep in mind for clutching some situations the next thing in regard to your e is that you don't have to immediately jump in when you land it good at Kali players will often wait before pressing their E2 to gather information or wait for cooldowns like in this fight for example akali lands e onto the enemy Gangplank but doesn't immediately take it her immediate thought process should be to stall as long as possible to buy time for her W to come back up but by waiting she also notices that Gangplank is moving away from his own teammates which gives her an even better opportunity to jump in and finish him off it's not just cooldowns that stalling is good for you can wait for energy or even some health regen before committing here akali lands e on Echo and knows she has to Tower dive for the kill so she Min Maxes her door and shield regen before taking E2 this way she got barely enough Health to survive the dive and live with literally 2 Hp in any case the weight can be for basically anything sometimes you just want to get the Canon minion before you go in for the kill doesn't matter what it is just don't feel like you have to immediately take your dash every single time another vital tip to keep in mind is that you can get two proxy or passive with both parts of your e this is way more relevant during the early game where you have much more limited damage you should try to make an effort to not double tap your e in close fights instead use E Auto E2 and auto again to maximize your DPS finally as a quick reminder akali's E2 has no max range this means that you can even follow Champions who use their Global ultimates to get around the map if doesn't happen often but there is definitely a bit more value to picking akali when there's a global on the enemy team such as Shen or Twisted Fate now that we've got her e fully covered let's break down her R the biggest tip you need to keep in mind comes when using her R2 it actually has a fairly decent hitbox behind akali so you don't always have to dash through your opponent to execute them but instead you can often Dash away to safety while killing them at the same time a ton of akali's kills will be done with backward R's so make sure you don't forget this next remember that both your R1 and R2 travel a fixed distance you can't shorten either in any way so here's how you can play around it for your R1 you can often use this to reposition a safety here akali is caught out by the enemy of Mumu as she sits in her shroud she thinks about her options and decides to use her R through the Gangplank positioning her perfectly to retreat the enemy amumu gets upset that she's getting away and over chases giving akali a kill on her way out too not only can you kite away with it but using R1 to get closer to your main kill Target is perfect as well using your opponent's teammates to get to who you actually want to kill is something you'll do quite a bit it's especially important in later team fights where the enemy carries hide behind their team R through their front line and you'll have a much easier time accessing the squishy enemy carries now when it comes to R2 you also have to play around the set travel distance the main way of doing this is usually walking backwards so right at this moment if akali keeps chasing then when vlad's pool runs out using R2 would put her a bit deep into the enemy Tower it's a bit subtle but pay attention when she waits out his pool she'll walk backward a bit and maintains this exact range now from this range she can alt it any direction that Vlad runs towards and only be slightly in Tower range the final thing to note is that you could do some fancy stuff with r and Flash like this R1 flash combo to deal damage to another Target or you can Flash during your R2 for the damage it's nice to know these combos but they're not really used Too Much finally as far as your W goes it's a stealth needless to say it's pretty op just putting it down will immediately give you value as it's really frustrating for your opponents to play against as for what you can do with it remember that you can take that time to reassess fights look at what cooldowns you have available who is near you where to move towards Etc there's also the obvious mechanic you'll immediately start using where you can leave your e in your W this is a great tool for when you want to go in and score a kill or just buy time all while having a reliable Escape Route for some other applications though remember that in Lane it's a great tool for dropping Minion aggro Minions are a huge source of damage early on so being able to trade into big waves as akali and not take any minion harass is a great tool you want to take advantage of for our last W tip you can actually Channel your teleport while shrouded this may potentially get you out of a sticky situation versus Champions with no AOE CC although it's rare it can definitely be a lifesaver okay with all the most important tips you need to know out of the way let's briefly cover what you should look out for during the laning phase keep in mind that akali is one of the weakest level 1 champions in the game due to her Q's super high energy cost costing 130 means that you'd have to wait for 60 energy to cast a second Q which is definitely not happening all this means is that you're playing fairly passively from levels one to three but that's the general game plan for most assassins so this isn't anything new keep in mind though that each point you put in your queue reduces the energy cost by a fair amount this means that your biggest spikes in the laning phase will come at levels four five seven and nine basically every time you put a point in Q that's because with each level you'll be able to incorporate more and more cues with every trade as for what your goals in Lane are most akali players tend to go for an attrition based style early on this is because of how good akali is at using the combination of doran's shield and second wind although you have no innate sustain this combo is absurdly op on akali and if it hasn't been nerfed you'll be running it almost every single game that's because akali can force very short trades with their W basic whenever she wants so what you'll be doing is slowly baiting your opponents into wasting their health and Mana with every trade try to keep these trades fairly short early on remember your goal is to slightly chunk them and to passively regenerate a ton of HP by doing so you'll eventually Whittle someone down enough to either force them out of lane or kill them once you're level six though here's what you want to do try to keep the wave closer to your side of the lane akali doesn't have a ton of immediate bursts during the early levels generally she wants to go for slightly longer almonds so keeping the wave on your side of the lane gives you way more time to chase down and DPS your opponent before they can get to the safety of their Tower as far as team fighting goes you have so much Mobility that reaching enemy carries should be a breeze You're playing an assassin so killing squishy targets is your main goal in fights shocking we know there is one extra thing we want to cover though do not underestimate the value you get by just sitting in stealth in the middle of the enemy team unlike other assassins who have to go in and out of fights akali can just sit there in the middle of it and be very threatening your opponents have to respect you which gives your teammates the time to catch up and reinforce course your play or to kill other targets it sounds silly but just sitting there and stealth menacingly is honestly one of the best ways that higher ELO akali's contribute to team fights and of course once you see an opportunity you come out and annihilate someone alright guys let's finish things off with a quick starter build these are the runes you'll be running most of the time akali makes very very advantages and how do i punish the enemy with them if we can ask the first question now the thing we can confidently say is that we have a time advantage and the second question can be answered with a few different things one we can push minions while the enemy can't and two we can take control of any space however we can notice that bran doesn't do either of these things he stands behind his minions and that's it i want to take note of one more thing before you jump into the challenger gameplay which is where everyone is standing not only is brand not threatening anything it's not like anyone else in this lane is either three minions are dead and no one's even touched each other or really touched the wave and just the time it takes for three minions to die let me show you how i can asks if aphelios knows the correct answer to do here and then he also has to make the correct follow-up in regards to the wave it's the same timer as in the previous game but lots of things are existing let's focus on thresh again now he's going to move up and try to take control of as much space as possible to try and win the war over the wave he baits out bart's q with good spacing and like how in chess two grand masters are going to be basically making good moves for the vast majority of the game well if a grandmaster plays against someone at a thousand elo they're going to win not because the grand master will play different better moves but because the 1000 elo player will not know how to respond to them in the same way that the grandmaster did again it's a test now i want to highlight that thresh is also asking the question of what is my advantage right now and to be honest it might not be clear but as he hits the wave it should become obvious that his relic stacks that he saved all this time were exactly what he was playing around now he's got a significant push advantage and immediately can look to move up to take advantage of the incoming level 2. he zones ophelius and bard completely off of the wave and this is just beautifully played from both sides notice that not only was thresh super good at realizing what his advantage actually was the opponents were very good at realizing their state of disadvantage and backing off before any serious punish could happen to them this recognition of advantage and disadvantage is the hallmark of high elo and it's why there are so fewer kills as you climb but the kills that do happen matter way more not only are players better at avoiding mistakes they're better at punishing their leads as well let's just jump back into the low replay to see the difference while thresh and bard were both very skilled at recognizing their states of advantage and disadvantage the same can't be said here brand really titre: 3 Minute Shaco Guide - A Guide for League of Legends
transcription: Why play Shako Top? Well, he deals massive AoE damage. He has amazing outplay potential. He's very difficult to ever actually kill, and he's just frustrating to play against. However, he can take some time to learn, and he doesn't always fit well into a team comp. Shako's passive are his basic attacks are empowered when hitting an enemy from behind to deal bonus damage, and both his Q and his E have additional effects when hitting an enemy from behind. Shako's Q is one of the most ridiculous abilities in the game. It causes him to blink in a direction and become invisible. The invisibility ends after a set amount of time or when he attacks or uses E. However, he can use his W and ultimate while still invisible. After breaking invisibility, his next attack deals bonus damage, and this is massively increased if hit from behind. Shako's W places down a box, which becomes invisible and armed after 2 seconds. If an enemy walks nearby, the box activates, fearing enemies, rooting them and dealing constant damage. And if the box is only attacking one target, it deals massively increased damage. Shako's E has a passive where if the ability is not on cooldown, all of his attacks slow. The active is a point-and-click ability which deals damage to the target hit and slows them. And if they're below 30% health, it deals massively increased damage. In addition, it deals even more damage if the dagger hits them from behind. Shako's ultimate causes him to vanish for half a second and blink in a direction, creating a clone of himself. He can then recast this ability to move the clone around. This clone can attack enemies and mimic his abilities. And after 18 seconds or when it dies, it explodes into three boxes which attack and fear enemies. For combo, Shaker can do a lot of fun things. A good go-to is to Q behind your opponent and use W. Attack away, hitting them from behind as much as possible, ending with E. When your clone has low health or duration, run it at your opponent and Q behind them using W. Attack as your clone explodes and then your W goes off, essentially double fearing them, and then attack away, ending with E. And another fun combo is to alt and Q behind your opponent and use W. Attack with your clone until W procs, then attack yourself from behind, ending with E. For matchups, Shako's actually great into range top laners, easily getting onto them. And he can struggle against champions who don't let him make fun plays. For runes, comet is great extra poke damage. For build order, start ring and pot into a black fire torch and tier 2 boots. From here, Alandre and Shadow Flame are great pickups before finishing a build with any of these. For skill order, start W, then Q, then E before maxing W, then E, then Q, taking alt whenever you can. For summoner spells, take teleport and ignite. Your Q is essentially a free flash anyway. Start in the game, poke away and look for opportunities to trade. Your E is point-and-click damage, which also procs comet. With your Q, you can essentially choose to fight whenever you want to. So, especially if your opponent's on cooldown, drop a box behind them and let them have it. Otherwise, set yourself up with safe areas with W and force your opponent to chase you into them. Entering the mid game, take tower and split push with pressure. This will give your team a lot of map presence and you're basically impossible to catch in a side lane where you can then just group up and assassinate a carry. Entering late game team fights, trap areas around objectives. Your boxes deal so much damage. If they have to chase you through, then they just die. Otherwise, play the disruptive assassin. Q onto the back line, set a trap for the carries, and clone yourself. Deal massive damage while relying on deception for safety.

titre: How to Manipulate RNG in League of Legends's 2v2 Arena Mode | Sweaty Nidhogg Guides
transcription: in League arena there are four copies of each augment in the pool this number is derived from the earlier iterations of Arena where they limited augments to two copies with eight players now that there are 16 players we can use common sense and assume that there's a limit of four copies instead I have personally seen four copies but never five so I don't know for a fact but from what I can tell this is true in order to explain how to take advantage of this I'm going to try and simplify it with a deck of cards more specifically just the face cards and the aces four copies of each let's also play as if there are only two players you and your opponent this smaller deck represents the pool of augments and for Simplicity sake let's assume you can also draw copies of the same augment this cannot happen in Arena but that's to your benefit so ignore that part for now let's play you draw three augments and they draw three augments let's say we're looking for an ace if we get it we take it otherwise we're going to try and reroll for it but the thing is time is a factor every round both players get about 40 seconds to make their decisions this is important to remember because this could quickly become a meta of who can read faster as a result of this video sorry let's continue it looks like you didn't get an ace but your opponent did you don't know that though so to roll into let's run that back but with the appropriate strategy suppose your opponent draws an ace again while you don't you don't know what he has but there's a crucial moment in time where you or complaining in general unless you play like 51 games a pike then you can complain so I can already hear the counter ments I'm going to address the main one yes you don't know if the opponent had an ace to shovel back it could have just been things you don't want and it would add to the pool of not Aces as in things you don't want this is correct however in the context of League the odds that in a pool of 48 augments on screen with duplicates of course as well as people rrolling back and fourth that a single person doesn't have an ace is pretty unlikely to put into perspective there are 40 prismatics 55 Golds and 51 Silvers to the best of my knowledge even with duplicates the entire Lobby will probably have at least half of the available augments on screen at any given moment this means that someone out there is probably holding your Ace so the moment 15 people show face up that they're not holding the ace you know for a fact that there are four copies for you to run into Which is less variance despite the fluff going back into the pool than trying to guess how many people have copies of the Ace in their hand right now you could technically try to roll for something while literally four people are staring at it on their screen and they're just like waiting to make a decision you know what I mean the game won't tell you that you had technically 0% chance so you're best bet is to generally wait for information all right so now we have an edge case unique augments or conditional augments such Stosur skill sniper or like even draven's exclusive augment are examples of things you can know for a fact whether or not people are holding them for example if you somehow get into a lobby with 15 other melee Champions and your range character looking for dryer sword everyone else is basically holding for you because that's 15 players times three augments each 45 copies of not draw your sword being held which means you need to roll right now immediately another example of Stosur is if you're the only stacking champion in the lobby [ __ ] roll right now if there's another Vega or some [ __ ] you're just hoping he selects his augmen quickly and shows not stosa orus face up while everyone else is holding their augs for you but of course your opponents aren't going to collude with you so it's a game of speed and checking everyone quickly really really cool one would be to take an augment that replaces your Summoner spell because the moment you do it it eliminates all the other Summoner spell augments this ever so slightly Narrows the odds for you in particular if you're not looking for [ __ ] like orbital strike or feel the burn there are so many different ways to take advantage of exclusivity and augment rules these are a couple of them I'm not going to State them all but you can think of yourself and you know hopefully this helps before we move on to the final topic Prismatic items are not unique amongst the whole lobby but for individual players they are there are 36 prismatics and you can't roll the same one again so if you roll two that you like don't make the mistake of assuming that you can get it again unless you buy like 12
